commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3d22e44669f1f43087daf344dd5b113011715413)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds opt-in `sync --stale-threshold` stale detection, force-close/reconnect handling, a `HEARTBEAT` store file, `doctor` `last_activity_at`, docs, changelog entries, and tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence end-to-end reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows current main lacks a connected-but-stale detection path, and the linked report includes live logs, but the actual stale WhatsApp stream was not reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor final-head live proof and maintainer acceptance of the new stale-threshold, heartbeat, and doctor surfaces; automation cannot provide the contributor's WhatsApp runtime proof.

target.title: fix: add stale-stream detection to sync --follow
target.body:
## What changed

Add built-in stale detection so `sync --follow` automatically reconnects when the WhatsApp connection is alive but no events are arriving.

### Changes

- **`--stale-threshold` flag** on `sync` command (default: 0 = disabled). When set, the follow loop checks whether any events have been received within the threshold window; if not, it emits a `stale` NDJSON event and forces a reconnect via `ReconnectWithBackoff`.
- **`HEARTBEAT` file** written to the store directory (throttled to once per minute) on every event. This lets external processes monitor sync freshness without querying the database.
- **`store.last_activity_at`** in `doctor --json` output, read from the heartbeat file. Enables automation to distinguish "connected but stale" from healthy.

### Why

Issue #268: `wacli sync --follow` can remain active/connected under systemd while the local store freshness stops advancing. Downstream automations miss messages even though the sync service appears healthy.

### How it was tested

- `TestSyncFollowReconnectsWhenStaleThresholdExceeded`: verifies follow mode reconnects when no events arrive for the stale threshold duration
- `TestSyncFollowDoesNotReconnectWhenActivityIsRecent`: verifies activity resets the stale timer and prevents spurious reconnects
- `TestSyncWritesHeartbeatFileOnActivity`: verifies the heartbeat file is written during sync
- `TestReadHeartbeatReturnsZeroForMissingFile` / `TestReadHeartbeatReturnsTimestampFromFile`: verifies heartbeat read logic
- `TestSyncFollowEmitsStaleEvent`: verifies the `stale` NDJSON event is emitted
- `TestDoctorReportsLastActivityFromHeartbeat`: verifies doctor reports `last_activity_at` from the heartbeat file
- Full gate: `go test ./...`, `go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `gofmt`, `go build`

### Usage

```bash
# Auto-reconnect when no events arrive for 10 minutes
wacli sync --follow --stale-threshold 10m

# Monitor freshness
wacli doctor --json | jq '.data.store.last_activity_at'
```

### New flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--stale-threshold` | `0` (disabled) | Force reconnect when no events arrive for this long in follow mode |

### New event

| Event | Data | When |
|-------|------|------|
| `stale` | `{threshold, idle_duration}` | Stale threshold exceeded, about to reconnect |

Closes #268
target.author: thedavidweng

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
